title: Anavinga ilicifolia
intro: Anavinga ilicifolia, also known as the New Zealand holly, is a small, deciduous shrub native to New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues such as arthritis, wounds, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Anavinga ilicifolia include glycosides, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Maori medicine, Anavinga ilicifolia is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal remedies such as creams and ointments for skin conditions and wound healing.
